name: oraqle

metadata.name: oraqle
metadata.summary: Secure computation compiler for homomorphic encryption and arithmetic circuits in general

documentation:
A secure computation compiler

The oraqle compiler lets you generate arithmetic circuits from high-level Python code. It also lets you generate code using HElib. This repository uses a fork of fhegen as a dependency and adapts some of the code from [fhegen](https://github.com/Crypto-TII/fhegen), which was written by Johannes Mono, Chiara Marcolla, Georg Land, Tim Güneysu, and Najwa Aaraj. You can read their theoretical work at: https://eprint.iacr.org/2022/706. See [our documentation](https://jelle-vos.nl/oraqle) for more details. ## Setting up The best way to get things up and running is using a virtual environment: - Set up a virtualenv using `python3 -m venv venv` in the directory. - Enter the virtual environment using `source venv/bin/activate`. - Install the requirements using `pip install requirements.txt`. - *To overcome import problems*, run `pip install -e .`, which will create links to your files (so you do not need to re-install after every change). We are currently setting up documentation to be rendered using GitHub Actions.

documentation: Module containing fixed nodes: nodes with a fixed number of inputs.

documentation: Overwrites the operands of this node.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.fixed.FixedNode.operation
documentation: Evaluates this node on the specified operands.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.fixed.FixedNode.arithmetize_depth_aware

A node with a fixed number of operands.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.fixed.BinaryNode
documentation: A node with two operands.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.non_commutative
documentation: A collection of abstract nodes representing operations that are non-commutative.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.non_commutative.NonCommutativeBinaryNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.non_commutative.NonCommutativeBinaryNode.__init__
documentation: Initialize a Node that performs an operation between two operands that is not commutative.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.non_commutative.NonCommutativeBinaryNode._operation_inner
documentation: Applies the binary operation on x and y.
Represents a non-cummutative binary operation such as `x < y` or `x - y`.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic
documentation: Module containing binary arithmetic nodes: additions and multiplications between non-constant nodes.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.CommutativeBinaryNode

documentation: Initialize the binary node with operands `left` and `right`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.CommutativeBinaryNode._operation_inner
documentation: Applies the binary operation on x and y.
This node has two operands and implements a commutative operation between arithmetic nodes.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.CommutativeArithmeticBinaryNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.CommutativeArithmeticBinaryNode.__init__
documentation:
Initialize this binary node with the given `left` and `right` operands.

Raises:
    Exception: Neither `left` nor `right` is allowed to be a `Constant`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.CommutativeArithmeticBinaryNode.squarings
documentation: TODO: Squaring should probably be a UniveriateNode
This node has two operands and implements a commutative operation between arithmetic nodes.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.Addition

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.Addition.__init__
documentation: Initialize a modular addition between `left` and `right`.
Performs modular addition of two previous nodes in an arithmetic circuit.
FIXME: This order should probably change

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.Multiplication

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.Multiplication.__init__
documentation: Initialize a modular multiplication between `left` and `right`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.binary_arithmetic.Multiplication.arithmetize
documentation: TODO: This is very hacky! Arithmetic nodes should simply not have to be arithmetized...
Performs modular multiplication of two previous nodes in an arithmetic circuit.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible
documentation: Module containing nodes with a flexible number of operands.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible.FlexibleNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible.FlexibleNode.arithmetize
documentation: TODO: Ensure that when all inputs are constants, the node is replaced with its evaluation
A node with an arbitrary number of operands. The operation must be reducible using a binary associative operation.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible.CommutativeUniqueReducibleNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible.CommutativeUniqueReducibleNode.__init__
documentation: Initialize a node with the given set as the operands. None of the operands can be a constant.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible.CommutativeUniqueReducibleNode._inner_operation
documentation: Perform the reducible operation performed by this node (order should not matter).

A node with an operation that is reducible without taking order into account: i.e. it has a binary operation that is associative and commutative.

The operands are unique, i.e. the same operand will never appear twice.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible.CommutativeMultiplicityReducibleNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.flexible.CommutativeMultiplicityReducibleNode.__init__
documentation: Initialize a reducible node with the given `Counter` representing the operands, none of which is allowed to be a constant.
A node with an operation that is reducible without taking order into account: i.e. it has a binary operation that is associative and commutative.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes
documentation: The nodes package contains a collection of fundamental abstract and concrete nodes.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.univariate
documentation: Abstract nodes for univariate operations.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.univariate.UnivariateNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.univariate.UnivariateNode._node_shape
documentation: Graphviz node shape.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.univariate.UnivariateNode.__init__
documentation: Initialize a univariate node.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.univariate.UnivariateNode._operation_inner
documentation: Evaluate the operation on the input. This method does not have to cache.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.univariate.UnivariateNode.is_equivalent
documentation:
Check whether `self` is semantically equivalent to `other`.

This function may have false negatives but it should never return false positives.

Returns:
-------
    `True` if `self` is semantically equivalent to `other`, `False` if they are not or that they cannot be shown to be equivalent.
An abstract node with a single input.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.leafs
documentation: Module containing leaf nodes: i.e. nodes without an input.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.leafs.ArithmeticLeafNode
documentation: An ArithmeticLeafNode is an ArithmeticNode with no inputs.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.leafs.Input

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.leafs.Input.__init__
documentation: Initialize an input with the given `name`.
Represents a named input to the arithmetic circuit.
TODO: Merge ArithmeticInput and Input using multiple inheritance

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.leafs.Constant

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.leafs.Constant.__init__
documentation: Initialize a Node with the given `value`.
Represents a Node with a constant value.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.leafs.DummyNode
documentation: A DummyNode is a fixed node with no inputs and no behavior.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic
documentation: This module contains arithmetic operations between a flexible number of inputs: summations and products.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic._generate_addition_tree
documentation: TODO: This is mostly copied from generate_multiplication_tree (depth is different)

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic.sum_
documentation:
Performs a sum between any number of nodes (or operands such as integers).

Returns:
    A `Sum` between all operands.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic.product_
documentation:
Performs a product between any number of nodes (or operands such as integers).

Returns:
    A `Product` between all operands.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic.Sum

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic.Sum.add_flatten
documentation:
Adds this node to `other`, flattening the summation if either of the two is also a `Sum` and absorbing `Constant`s.

Returns:
    A `Sum` node containing the flattened summation, or a `Constant` node.
This node represents a sum between two or more operands, or at least one operand and a constant.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic.Product

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic.Product.__init__
documentation: Initialize a `Product` with the given `Counter` as operands and an optional `constant`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.arbitrary_arithmetic.Product.mul_flatten
documentation:
Multiplies this node with `other`, flattening the product if either of the two is also a `Product` and absorbing `Constant`s.

Returns:
    A `Product` node containing the flattened product, or a `Constant` node.
This node represents a product between two or more operands, or at least one operand and a constant.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.unary_arithmetic
documentation: This module contains `ArithmeticNode`s with a single input: Constant additions and constant multiplications.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.unary_arithmetic.ConstantAddition

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.unary_arithmetic.ConstantAddition.__init__
documentation: Represents the operation `constant + node`.
This node represents a multiplication of another node with a constant.
TODO: There is (going to be) a lot of code duplication between these two classes

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.unary_arithmetic.ConstantMultiplication

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.unary_arithmetic.ConstantMultiplication.__init__
documentation: Represents the operation `constant * node`.
This node represents a multiplication of another node with a constant.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison
documentation: Package containing tools for expressing equality and comparison operations.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.in_upper_half
documentation: This module contains classes for checking if an element is in the upper half of the finite field.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.in_upper_half.test_evaluate_mod7
documentation: TODO: Make a univariate node class with an easy way to test if evaluation corresponds to evaluating the arithmetic

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.in_upper_half.InUpperHalf
documentation:
Returns 1 when the input is contained in the upper half of the field, which are considered the negative numbers.

Specifically, it returns 0 in the range [0, (p - 1) / 2] and 1 in the range ((p - 1) / 2, p - 1].

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.in_upper_half.IliashenkoZuccaInUpperHalf
documentation:
Returns 1 when the input is contained in the upper half of the field, which are considered the negative numbers.

Specifically, it returns 0 in the range [0, (p - 1) / 2] and 1 in the range ((p - 1) / 2, p - 1].

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.func2poly
documentation: Tools for interpolating polynomials from arbitrary functions.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.func2poly.principal_character
documentation:
Computes the principal character. This expression always returns 1 when x = 0 and 0 otherwise. Only works for prime moduli.

Returns:
    The principal character x**(p-1).

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.func2poly.interpolate_polynomial
documentation:
Interpolates a polynomial for the given function. This is currently only implemented for prime moduli. This function interpolates the polynomial on all possible inputs.

Returns:
    A sympy `Poly` object representing the unique polynomial that evaluates to the same outputs for all inputs as `function`.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.equality
documentation: This module contains classes for representing equality checks.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.equality.IsNonZero
documentation: This node represents a zero check: x == 0.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.equality.Equals
documentation: This node represents an equality operation: x == y.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.control_flow
documentation: This package contains control flow functions.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.polynomials
documentation:
The polynomials package contains nodes for performing polynomial evaluation.

In a finite field, the set of polyfunctions is the same as the set of all functions.
So, you can perform any function by interpolating a polynomial.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract
documentation: Module containing the most fundamental classes in the compiler.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.select_stack_index
documentation:
Selects a free index in the stack and occupies it.

Returns:
    The first free index in `stack_occupied`.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.iterate_increasing_depth
documentation:
Iterates over two ParetoFronts, returning pairs of ArithmeticNodes such that the multiplicative depth grows monotonically.

Yields:
    Pairs of tuples, containing the multiplicative depth, the multiplicative size/cost, and the arithmetization, in that order.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.try_to_node
documentation:
Tries to cast this object into a valid `Node`.

This can be used to transform e.g. an `int` or `bool` into a `Constant`.
If it is applied to a `Node`, it does nothing.

Returns:
A `Node` or `None` depending on whether the object is castable.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.__init__
documentation: Initialize an empty ParetoFront.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.from_node
documentation:
Initialize a `ParetoFront` with one node in it.

Returns:
    New `ParetoFront`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.from_leaf
documentation:
Initialize a `ParetoFront` with one leaf node in it.

Returns:
    New `ParetoFront`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.add
documentation:
Adds the given `Node` to the `ParetoFront` by computing its multiplicative depth and value.

Alternatively, the user can supply an unchecked `depth` and `value` so that these values do not have to be (re)computed.

Returns:
`True` if and only if the node was inserted into the ParetoFront (so it was in some way better than the current `Nodes`).

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront._add
documentation: Returns True if and only if the node was inserted into the ParetoFront.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.add_leaf
documentation: Add a leaf node to this `ParetoFront`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.add_front
documentation: Add all elements from `front` to `self`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.get_smallest_at_depth
documentation: Returns the circuit with the smallest value that has at most depth `max_depth`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.is_empty
documentation: Returns whether the front is empty.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ParetoFront.get_lowest_value
documentation: Returns the value (size or cost) of the Node with the highest depth, and therefore the lowest value.

Abstract base class for ParetoFronts.

One objective is to minimize the multiplicative depth, while the other objective is minimizing some value, such as the multiplicative size or cost.
TODO: It would be great if we can move out this ParetoFront class, but it's hard to do without circular imports

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.SizeParetoFront

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.SizeParetoFront.add
documentation:
Adds the given `Node` to the `SizeParetoFront` by computing its multiplicative depth and size.

Alternatively, the user can supply an unchecked `depth` and `size` so that these values do not have to be (re)computed.

Returns:
`True` if and only if the node was inserted into the ParetoFront (so it was in some way better than the current `Nodes`).
A `ParetoFront` that trades off multiplicative depth with multiplicative size.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.CostParetoFront

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.CostParetoFront.__init__
documentation: Initialize an empty `CostParetoFront` with the given `cost_of_squaring`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.CostParetoFront.from_node
documentation:
Initialize a `CostParetoFront` with one node in it.

Returns:
    New `CostParetoFront`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.CostParetoFront.from_leaf
documentation:
Initialize a `CostParetoFront` with one leaf node in it.

Returns:
    New `CostParetoFront`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.CostParetoFront.add
documentation:
Adds the given `Node` to the `CostParetoFront` by computing its multiplicative depth and cost.

Alternatively, the user can supply an unchecked `depth` and `cost` so that these values do not have to be (re)computed.

Returns:
`True` if and only if the node was inserted into the ParetoFront (so it was in some way better than the current `Nodes`).
A `ParetoFront` that trades off multiplicative depth with multiplicative cost.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node._hash_name
documentation: TODO: This property should be removed if we do not provide a default hash implementation.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.__init__
documentation: Creates a new node, of which the result is known by the parties identified by `known_by`, as well as those who know all input operands.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.apply_function_to_operands
documentation: Applies function to all operands of this node.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.replace_operands_using_function
documentation: Replaces each operand of this node with the node generated by calling function on said operand.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.evaluate
documentation: Evaluates the node in the arithmetic circuit. The output should always be reduced modulo the modulus.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.clear_cache
documentation: Clears any cached values of the node and any of its operands.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.to_graph
documentation:
Adds this node to the graph as well as its edges.

Returns:
    The identifier of this `Node` in the `DotFile`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.is_equivalent
documentation:
Checks whether two nodes are semantically equivalent.

This method will always return `False` if they are not.
This method will maybe return True if they are indeed equivalent.
In other words, this method may produce false negatives, but it will never produce false positives.
TODO: We can add a strategy to this method, e.g. to exhaustively check equivalence.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.eliminate_common_subexpressions
documentation:
Eliminates duplicate subexpressions that are equivalent (as defined by a node's `__eq__` and `__hash__` method).

Returns:
    A `Node` that must replace the previous expression.
TODO: Rework CSE. In an arithmetic circuit, it should only return arithmetic nodes.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.count_parents
documentation: Counts the total number of nodes in this subcircuit.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.reset_parent_count
documentation: Resets the cached number of nodes in this subcircuit to 0.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.arithmetize
documentation:
Arithmetizes this node, replacing it with only arithmetic operations (constants, additions, and multiplications).

The current implementation only aims at reducing the total number of multiplications.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.arithmetize_depth_aware
documentation:
Arithmetizes this node in a depth-aware fashion, replacing high-level nodes with only arithmetic operations (constants, additions, and multiplications).

Returns:
    `CostParetoFront` containing a front that trades off multiplicative depth and multiplicative cost.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.to_arithmetic
documentation:
Outputs this node's equivalent ArithmeticNode. Errors if this node does not have a direct arithmetic equivalent.

Raises:
    Exception: If there is no direct arithmetic equivalent.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.add
documentation:
Performs a summation between `self` and `other`, possibly flattening any sums.

It is possible to disable flattening by setting `flatten=False`.

Returns:
    A possibly flattened `Sum` node or a `Constant` representing self & other.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.mul
documentation:
Performs a multiplication between `self` and `other`, possibly flattening any products.

It is possible to disable flattening by setting `flatten=False`.

Returns:
    A possibly flattened `Product` node or a `Constant` representing self & other.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.bool_or
documentation:
Performs an OR operation between `self` and `other`, possibly flattening the result into an OR operation between many operands.

It is possible to disable flattening by setting `flatten=False`.

Returns:
    A possibly flattened `Or` node or a `Constant` representing self & other.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.Node.bool_and
documentation:
Performs an AND operation between `self` and `other`, possibly flattening the result into an AND operation between many operands.

It is possible to disable flattening by setting `flatten=False`.

Returns:
    A possibly flattened `And` node or a `Constant` representing self & other.
Abstract node in an arithmetic circuit.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.UnoverloadedWrapper

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.UnoverloadedWrapper.__init__
documentation: Wrap `Node`.

The `UnoverloadedWrapper` class wraps a `Node` such that hash(.) and x == y work as expected.

!!! note
    The equality operator perform semantic equality!

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.clear_cache
documentation: Clears any cached values of the node and any of its operands.
ArithmeticNode should be like an interface; it should not have an __init__ method.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.operands
documentation: Returns the operands (children) of this node. The list can be empty. The nodes MUST be arithmetic nodes.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.set_operands
documentation: Overwrites the operands of this node. The nodes MUST be arithmetic nodes.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.multiplicative_depth
documentation:
Computes the multiplicative depth of this node and its children recursively.

Returns:
The largest number of multiplications from the output of this node to the leafs of this subcircuit.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.multiplicative_size
documentation:
Computes the multiplicative size (number of multiplications) by counting the size of the set returned by self.multiplications().

Returns:
The number of multiplications in this subcircuit.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.multiplicative_cost
documentation:
Computes the multiplicative cost (number of general multiplications + cost_of_squaring * squarings).

It does so by counting the size of the sets returned by self.multiplications() and self.squarings().

Returns:
    The number of proper multiplications + the cost of squaring * the number of squarings.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.multiplications
documentation:
Returns a set of all the multiplications in this tree of descendants, including itself.

This includes any squarings.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.squarings
documentation: Returns a set of all the squarings in this tree of descendants, including itself.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.nodes.abstract.ArithmeticNode.create_instructions
documentation:
Creates a set of instructions of this node to the given file. Returns the index in the stack of the output and the stack_counter.

!!! note
    This method assumes that the _parent_count of each node is up to date.

Extension of Node to indicate that this is a node permitted in a purely arithmetic circuit (with binary additions and multiplications).

The ArithmeticNode 'mixin' must always come before the base class in the class declaration.
TODO: Do we need a separate class to distinguish nodes from arithmetic nodes (which only have arithmetic operands)?

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.control_flow.conditional
documentation: This module contains tools for evaluating conditional statements.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.control_flow.conditional.if_else
documentation:
Sugar expression for creating an if-else clause.

Returns:
    An `IfElse` node that equals `positive` if `condition` is true, and `negative` otherwise.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.control_flow.conditional.IfElse

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.control_flow.conditional.IfElse.__init__
documentation: Initialize an if-else node: If condition evaluates to true, then it outputs positive, otherwise it outputs negative.
A node representing an if-else clause.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.poly2circuit
documentation:
Module for automatic circuit generation for any functions with any number of inputs.

Warning: These circuits can be very large!

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.poly2circuit.construct_subcircuit
documentation:
Build a circuit with a single output given an expression of simple arithmetic operations in Sympy.

Raises:
------
Exception: Exponents must be integers, or an exception will be raised.

Returns:
-------
A subcircuit (Node) computing the given sympy expression.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.poly2circuit.construct_circuit
documentation:
Construct an arithmetic circuit from a list of polynomials and the fixed modulus.

Returns:
-------
A circuit outputting the evaluation of each polynomial.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler
documentation: The compiler package contains the main machinery for describing high-level circuits, arithmetizing them, and generating code.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions
documentation: This module contains the classes that represent instructions and programs for evaluating arithmetic circuits.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticInstruction

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticInstruction.__init__
documentation: Initialize an instruction that writes it output to the stack at `stack_index`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticInstruction.evaluate
documentation: Executes the instruction on plaintext inputs without using encryption, keeping track of the plaintext values in the stack.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticInstruction.generate_code
documentation: Generates code for this instruction, keeping track of which places of the stack are already initialized.
An abstract arithmetic instruction that computes an operation in an arithmetic circuit using a stack.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.AdditionInstruction

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.AdditionInstruction.__init__
documentation: Initialize an instruction that adds the elements at `left_stack_index` and `right_stack_index`, placing the result at `stack_index`.
Reads two elements from the stack, adds them, and writes the result to the stack.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.MultiplicationInstruction

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.MultiplicationInstruction.__init__
documentation: Initialize an instruction that multiplies the elements at `left_stack_index` and `right_stack_index`, placing the result at `stack_index`.
Reads two elements from the stack, multiplies them, and writes the result to the stack.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ConstantAdditionInstruction

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ConstantAdditionInstruction.__init__
documentation: Initialize an instruction that adds `constant` to the element at `input_stack_index`, placing the result at `stack_index`.
Reads an element from the stack, adds a constant to it it, and writes the result to the stack.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ConstantMultiplicationInstruction

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ConstantMultiplicationInstruction.__init__
documentation: Initialize an instruction that multiplies the element at `input_stack_index` with `constant`, placing the result at `stack_index`.
Reads an element from the stack, multiplies it with a constant, and writes the result to the stack.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.InputInstruction

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.InputInstruction.__init__
documentation: Initialize an `InputInstruction` that places the input with the given `name` in the stack at index `stack_index`.
Writes an input to the stack.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.OutputInstruction
documentation: Outputs an element from the stack.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticProgram

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticProgram.__init__
documentation:
Initialize an `ArithmeticProgram` from a list of `instructions`.

The user must specify an upper bound on the `stack_size` required.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticProgram.execute
documentation:
Executes the arithmetic program on plaintext inputs without using encryption.

Raises:
    Exception: If there were no outputs in this program.
    
Returns:
The first output in this program.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.instructions.ArithmeticProgram.generate_code
documentation:
Generates HElib code for this program.

If `decrypt_outputs` is true, then the generated code will decrypt the outputs at the end of the circuit.

Returns:
    The generated code as a string.

An ArithmeticProgram represents an ordered set of arithmetic operations that compute an arithmetic circuit.

The easiest way to obtain an `ArithmeticProgram` of an `ArithmeticCircuit` is to call `ArithmeticCircuit.generate_program()`.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean
documentation: This package contains nodes for expressing common Boolean operations.

documentation: Evaluation of univariate polynomials.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.polynomials.univariate.UnivariatePoly.__init__
documentation: Initialize a univariate polynomial with the given coefficients from least to highest order.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.polynomials.univariate.UnivariatePoly.from_function
documentation:
Interpolate a univariate polynomial for the given function.

Returns:
-------
A UnivariatePoly whose coefficients compute the `function` on all inputs.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.polynomials.univariate.UnivariatePoly.arithmetize_custom
documentation:
Compute an arithmetization along with a dictionary of precomputed powers.

Returns:
-------
An arithmetization and a dictionary of previously computed powers.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.polynomials.univariate.UnivariatePoly.arithmetize_depth_aware_custom
documentation:
Compute a depth-aware arithmetization as well as a dictionary indexed by the depth of the nodes in the front. The dictionary stores precomputed powers.

Returns:
-------
A CostParetoFront with the depth-aware arithmetization and a dictionary indexed by the depth of the nodes in the front, returning a dictionary with previously computed powers.
Evaluation of a univariate polynomial.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison
documentation: Classes for representing comparisons such as x < y, x >= y, semi-comparisons etc.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.AbstractComparison

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.AbstractComparison.__init__
documentation: Initialize an abstract comparison, indicating the direction of the comparison by specifying `less_than`.
An abstract class for comparisons, representing that they can be flipped: i.e. x > y <=> y < x.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.SemiStrictComparison
documentation:
A node representing a comparison x < y or x > y that only works when x and y are at most p // 2 elements apart.

Semi-comparisons are only valid if the absolute difference between the inputs does not exceed half of the field size.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.StrictComparison
documentation: A node representing a comparison x < y or x > y.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.SemiComparison
documentation: A node representing a comparison x <= y or x >= y that only works when x and y are at most p // 2 elements apart.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.Comparison
documentation: A node representing a comparison x <= y or x >= y.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.T2SemiLessThan
documentation: Implementation of [the algorithm from the T2 framework](https://petsymposium.org/popets/2023/popets-2023-0075.pdf) for performing x < y.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.comparison.comparison.IliashenkoZuccaSemiLessThan
documentation: Implementation of the [Illiashenko-Zucca algorithm](https://eprint.iacr.org/2021/315) for performing x < y.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.graphviz.DotFile.add_node

Adds a node to the file. The keyword arguments are directly put into the DOT file.

For example, one can specify a label, a color, a style, etc...

Returns:

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.graphviz.DotFile.add_link
documentation: Adds an unformatted link between the nodes with `from_id` and `to_id`. The keyword arguments are directly put into the DOT file.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.graphviz.DotFile.to_file
documentation: Writes the DOT file to the given filename as a directed graph called 'G'.
A `DotFile` is a graph description format that can be rendered to e.g. PDF using graphviz.

Performs an OR operation with `other`, flattening the `Or` node if either of the two is also an `Or` and absorbing `Constant`s.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and
documentation: This module contains tools for evaluating AND operations between many inputs.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and._generate_sumred_tree
documentation: TODO: This is copied from arbitrary_arithmetic.py

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.minimize_depth_cost
documentation:
Finds the depth-cost Pareto front.

Returns:
    A front in the form of a list of tuples containing (depth, cost, node).

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.minimize_depth_cost_recursive
documentation:
Find a minimum-depth circuit for the given `breadth_limit` and `strict_cost_upper` bound.

Operands must be sorted from deep to shallow.
Returns the lowest-cost circuit for the given depth.
The exponentiation_specs must be sorted from high-cost to low-cost.

Returns:
    A minimum-depth circuit in the form of an `NaryLogicNode` satisfying the constraints, or None if the constraints cannot be satisfied.

functions.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.all_
documentation: Returns an `And` node that evaluates to true if any of the given `operands` evaluates to true.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.And

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.And.and_flatten
documentation:
Performs an AND operation with `other`, flattening the `And` node if either of the two is also an `And` and absorbing `Constant`s.

Returns:
    An `And` node containing the flattened AND operation, or a `Constant` node.
Performs an AND operation over several operands. The user must ensure that the operands are Booleans.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.NaryLogicNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.NaryLogicNode.local_cost

Represents a (sub)circuit for computing and AND or OR operation.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.InputNaryLogicNode

documentation: Initialize the input node with the given `breadth`.
An input logic node.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.ProductNaryLogicNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.ProductNaryLogicNode.__init__
documentation: Initialize a product subcircuit with the given `operands` and `breadth`.
A `ProductNaryLogicNode` represents an OR/AND (sub)circuit in which all inputs are multiplied (and flattened).

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.SumReduceNaryLogicNode

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.boolean.bool_and.SumReduceNaryLogicNode.__init__
documentation: Initialize a sum-reduce subcircuit with the given exponentiation chain (and properties), over the given `operands`.
A `SumReduceNaryLogicNode` represents an OR/AND (sub)circuit in which all inputs are summed and then reduced to a Boolean.

modules.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit
documentation: This module contains classes for representing circuits.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.__init__
documentation: Initialize a circuit with the given `outputs`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluates the circuit with the given named inputs.

This function does not error if it is given more inputs than necessary, but it will error if one is missing.

Returns:
    Evaluated output in plain text.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.to_graph
documentation: Saves a DOT file representing the circuit as a graph at the given `file_name`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.to_pdf
documentation: Saves a PDF file representing the circuit as a graph at the given `file_name`.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.to_svg

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.display_graph
documentation: Displays the circuit in a Python notebook.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.eliminate_subexpressions
documentation: Perform semantic common subexpression elimination on all outputs.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.is_equivalent
documentation:
Returns whether the two circuits are semantically equivalent.

False positives do not occure but false negatives do.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.arithmetize
documentation:
Arithmetizes this circuit by calling arithmetize on all outputs.

This replaces all high-level operations with arithmetic operations (constants, additions, and multiplications).
The current implementation only aims at reducing the total number of multiplications.

Returns:
    An equivalent arithmetic circuit with low multiplicative size.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.Circuit.arithmetize_depth_aware
documentation:
Perform depth-aware arithmetization on this circuit.

!!! failure
    The current implementation only supports circuits with a single output.

This function replaces high-level nodes with arithmetic operations (constants, additions, and multiplications).

Returns:
    A list with tuples containing the multiplicative depth, the multiplicative cost, and the generated arithmetization from low to high depth.
Represents a circuit over a fixed finite field that can be turned into an arithmetic circuit. Behind the scenes this is a directed acyclic graph (DAG). The circuit only has references to the outputs.

classes.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.ArithmeticCircuit

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.ArithmeticCircuit.multiplicative_depth
documentation: Returns the multiplicative depth of the circuit.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.ArithmeticCircuit.multiplicative_size
documentation: Returns the multiplicative size (number of multiplications) of the circuit.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.ArithmeticCircuit.multiplicative_cost
documentation: Returns the multiplicative cost of the circuit.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.ArithmeticCircuit.generate_program
documentation: Returns an arithmetic program for this arithmetic circuit.

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.ArithmeticCircuit.summands_between_multiplications
documentation:
Computes the maximum number of summands between two consecutive multiplications in this circuit.

!!! failure
    This currently returns the hardcoded value 10

Returns:
    The highest number of summands between two consecutive multiplications

methods.name: oraqle.compiler.circuit.ArithmeticCircuit.generate_code
documentation:
Generates an HElib implementation of the circuit.

If decrypt_outputs is True, prints the decrypted output.
Otherwise, it prints whether the ciphertext has noise budget remaining (i.e. it is correct with high probability).

!!! note
    Decryption is part of the measured run time.

Args:
    filename: Test
    iterations: Number of times to run the circuit
    measure_time: Whether to output a measurement of the total run time
    decrypt_outputs: Whether to print the decrypted outputs, or to simply check if there is noise budget remaining

Returns:
    Parameters that were chosen: (ring dimension m, Hensel lifting = 1, bits in the modchain, columns in key switching = 3).
Represents an arithmetic circuit over a fixed finite field, so it only contains arithmetic nodes.

modules.name: oraqle.circuits
documentation: This package contains example circuits and tools for generating them.

modules.name: oraqle.circuits.veto_voting
documentation:
The veto voting circuit is the inverse of a consensus vote between a number of participants.

The circuit is essentially a large OR operation, returning 1 if any participant vetoes (by submitting a 1).
This represents a vote that anyone can veto.

functions.name: oraqle.circuits.veto_voting.gen_veto_voting_circuit
documentation: Returns a veto voting circuit between the number of `participants`.

This decorator caches the calls to this function in a file on disk, ignoring the arguments listed in `ignore_args`.

Returns:
    A cached output
Adapted from: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/16463582/memoize-to-disk-python-persistent-memoization

modules.name: oraqle.add_chains
documentation: Tools for generating addition chains using different constraints and objectives.

modules.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_front
documentation: Tools for generating addition chains that trade off depth and cost.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_front.chain_depth
documentation: Return the depth of the addition chain.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_front.gen_pareto_front
documentation: Returns a Pareto front of addition chains, trading of cost and depth.

modules.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_mod
documentation: Tools for computing addition chains, taking into account the modular nature of the algebra.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_mod.hw
documentation: Returns the Hamming weight of n.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_mod.size_lower_bound
documentation: Returns a lower bound on the size of the addition chain for this target.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_mod.cost_lower_bound_monotonic
documentation: Returns a lower bound on the cost of the addition chain for this target. The bound is guaranteed to grow monotonically with the target.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_mod.chain_cost
documentation: Returns the cost of the addition chain, considering doubling (squaring) to be cheaper than other additions (multiplications).

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_mod.add_chain_modp
documentation:
Computes an addition chain for target modulo p with the given constraints and optimization parameters.

The precomputed_powers are an optional set of powers that have previously been computed along with their depth.
This means that those powers can be reused for free.

Returns:
    If it exists, a minimal addition chain meeting the given constraints and optimization parameters.

modules.name: oraqle.config
documentation:
This module contains global configuration options.

!!! warning
    This is almost certainly going to be removed in the future.
We do not want oraqle to have a global configuration, but this is currently an intentional evil to prevent large refactors in the initial versions.

variables.name: oraqle.config.MAXSAT_TIMEOUT
documentation:
Time-out for individual calls to the MaxSAT solver.

!!! danger
    This causes non-deterministic behavior!
    
!!! bug
    There is currently a chance to get `AttributeError`s, which is a bug caused by pysat trying to delete an oracle that does not exist.
    There is no current workaround for this.

variables.name: oraqle.config.PS_METHOD_FACTOR_K
documentation:
Approximation factor for the PS-method, higher is better.

The Paterson-Stockmeyer method takes a value k, that is theoretically optimal when k = sqrt(2 * degree).
However, sometimes it is better to try other values of k (e.g. due to rounding and to trade off depth and cost).
This factor, let's call it f, is used to limit the candidate values of k that we try: [1, f * sqrt(2 * degree)).

modules.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_heuristic
documentation: This module contains functions for finding addition chains, while sometimes resorting to heuristics to prevent long computations.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains_heuristic.add_chain_guaranteed
documentation:
Always generates an addition chain for a given target, which is suboptimal if the inputs are too large.

In some cases, the result is not necessarily optimal. These are the cases where we resort to a heuristic.
This currently happens if:
- The target exceeds 1000.
- The modulus (if provided) exceeds 200.
- MAXSAT_TIMEOUT is not None and a MaxSAT instance timed out

!!! note
    This function is useful for preventing long computation, but the result is not guaranteed to be (close to) optimal.
    Unlike `add_chain`, this function will always return an addition chain.

Parameters:
    target: The target integer.
    modulus: Modulus to take into account. In an exponentiation chain, this is the modulus in the exponent, i.e. x^target mod p corresponds to `modulus = p - 1`.
    squaring_cost: The cost of doubling (squaring), compared to other additions (multiplications), which cost 1.0.
    solver: Name of the SAT solver, e.g. "glucose421" for glucose 4.2.1. See: https://pysathq.github.io/docs/html/api/solvers.html.
    encoding: The encoding to use for cardinality constraints. See: https://pysathq.github.io/docs/html/api/card.html#pysat.card.EncType.
    thurber: Whether to use the Thurber bounds, which provide lower bounds for the elements in the chain. The bounds are ignored when `precomputed_values = True`.
    precomputed_values: If there are any precomputed values that can be used for free, they can be specified as a tuple of pairs (value, chain_depth).

Raises:  # noqa: DOC502
    TimeoutError: If the global MAXSAT_TIMEOUT is not None, and it is reached before a maxsat instance could be solved.

Returns:
    An addition chain.

modules.name: oraqle.add_chains.solving
documentation: Tools for solving SAT formulations.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.solving.solve
documentation:
This code is adapted from pysat's internal code to stop when we have reached a maximum cost.

Returns:
    A list containing the assignment (where 3 indicates that 3=True and -3 indicates that 3=False), or None if the wcnf is unsatisfiable.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.solving.extract_indices
documentation:
Returns the indices for each step of the addition chain.

If n precomputed values are provided, then these are considered to be the first n indices after x (i.e. x has index 0, followed by 1, ..., n representing the precomputed values).

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.solving.solve_with_time_limit
documentation:
This code is adapted from pysat's internal code to stop when we have reached a maximum cost.

Raises:
    TimeoutError: When a timeout occurs (after `timeout_secs` seconds)

Returns:
    A list containing the assignment (where 3 indicates that 3=True and -3 indicates that 3=False), or None if the wcnf is unsatisfiable.

modules.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains
documentation: Tools for generating short addition chains using a MaxSAT formulation.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains.thurber_bounds
documentation: Returns the Thurber bounds for a given target and a maximum size of the addition chain.

functions.name: oraqle.add_chains.addition_chains.add_chain
documentation:
Generates a minimum-cost addition chain for a given target, abiding to the constraints.

Parameters:
    target: The target integer.
    max_depth: The maximum depth of the addition chain
    strict_cost_max: A strict upper bound on the cost of the addition chain. I.e., cost(chain) < strict_cost_max.
    squaring_cost: The cost of doubling (squaring), compared to other additions (multiplications), which cost 1.0.
    solver: Name of the SAT solver, e.g. "glucose421" for glucose 4.2.1. See: https://pysathq.github.io/docs/html/api/solvers.html.
    encoding: The encoding to use for cardinality constraints. See: https://pysathq.github.io/docs/html/api/card.html#pysat.card.EncType.
    thurber: Whether to use the Thurber bounds, which provide lower bounds for the elements in the chain. The bounds are ignored when `precomputed_values = True`.
    min_size: The minimum size of the chain. It is always possible to use `math.ceil(math.log2(target))`.
    precomputed_values: If there are any precomputed values that can be used for free, they can be specified as a tuple of pairs (value, chain_depth).

Raises:  # noqa: DOC502
    TimeoutError: If the global MAXSAT_TIMEOUT is not None, and it is reached before a maxsat instance could be solved.

Returns:
    A minimum-cost addition chain, if it exists.
